mission.title: rook: ceph: Mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim
mission.description: ceph: Mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your rook deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
Bug Report

**Current Behaviour:**
ceph status return "HEALTH_WARN" 
with reason: mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim

ceph status return "HEALTH_OK" 

Upgraded Ceph from 1.4 -> 1.5 -> 1.6.1
All OK.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ceph: Mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim
steps.description:
I can confirm this is happening in all clusters, whether a clean install or upgraded cluster, running at least versions: v14.2.20, v15.2.11 or v16.2.1. 

According to the [CVE](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/security/CVE-2021-20288/) also previously mentioned, there is a security issue where
```yaml
NEW_CEPH_IMAGE='ceph/ceph:v15.2.11-20210419'
CLUSTER_NAME="$ROOK_CLUSTER_NAMESPACE"  # change if your cluster name is not the Rook namespace
kubectl -n $ROOK_CLUSTER_NAMESPACE patch CephCluster $CLUSTER_NAME --type=merge -p "{\"spec\": {\"cephVersion\": {\"image\": \"$NEW_CEPH_IMAGE\"}}}"
```

steps.title: Upgrade rook to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade rook:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade rook rook/rook --namespace rook
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook
helm list -n rook
```

steps.title: Confirm ceph: Mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I can confirm this is happening in all clusters, whether a clean install or upgraded cluster, running at least versions: v14.2.20, v15.2.11 or v16.2.1. 

According to the [CVE](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/security/CVE-2021-20288/) also previously mentioned, there is a security issue where clients need to be upgraded to the releases mentioned. Once all the clients are updated (e.g.
codeSnippets:
NEW_CEPH_IMAGE='ceph/ceph:v15.2.11-20210419'
CLUSTER_NAME="$ROOK_CLUSTER_NAMESPACE"  # change if your cluster name is not the Rook namespace
kubectl -n $ROOK_CLUSTER_NAMESPACE patch CephCluster $CLUSTER_NAME --type=merge -p "{\"spec\": {\"cephVersion\": {\"image\": \"$NEW_CEPH_IMAGE\"}}}"

cluster:
    id:     3bd7a445-c9b4-4524-b298-b96d25e09251
    health: HEALTH_WARN
            mons are allowing insecure global_id reclaim

  services:
    mon: 3 daemons, quorum e,h,j (age 56m)
    mgr: a(active, since 55m)
    osd: 3 osds: 3 up (since 106m), 3 in (since 2w)

  data:

    objects: 4.51k objects, 15 GiB

    pgs:     33 active+clean

  io:
    client:   69 KiB/s wr, 0 op/s rd, 5 op/s wr

health: HEALTH_WARN
            client is using insecure global_id reclaim
            mon is allowing insecure global_id reclaim

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
